rationale_for_baseline_tier: Although the picture strongly suggests hyperventilation/anxiety on a benign early-B12 background, an older adult (70) with acute dizziness and sensory symptoms that cannot be examined remotely warrants same-day in-person assessment (T3) to exclude a posterior-circulation stroke, with a firm stroke safety-net escalating to T5 (000) if any focal feature appears. Remote-only reassurance is unsafe at this age.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unilateral weakness/facial droop/slurred speech, persistent vertigo at rest, double vision, dysphagia, severe headache, or blackout
escalation_edges.rationale: Would indicate acute stroke requiring emergency ambulance.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get weakness or drooping on one side, slurred speech, double vision, a severe headache, trouble swallowing, or you black out, call 000 straight away.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the threshold for in-person assessment and stroke exclusion.
Spouse can monitor, assist mobilising to reduce falls risk, and arrange transport.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Your B12 level is only mildly low and does not cause sudden nerve or spinal damage — the tingling is from breathing fast, which we can settle. But because you're 70 and had sudden dizziness I can't examine over video, please be seen in person today to be safe. Rise slowly, breathe slowly, and call 000 if you get one-sided weakness, slurred speech, double vision, or a blackout.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day in-person assessment (GP/urgent care/ED); 000 for stroke features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Attend ED tonight if you cannot be seen same-day or any stroke feature appears.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of hyperventilation/anxiety with bilateral paraesthesiae on an early-pernicious-anaemia background; stroke screened negative on history but not examinable remotely; same-day in-person assessment advised given age; stroke safety-net given; B12 replacement to be scheduled.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring an older adult with acute dizziness purely remotely as anxiety with no in-person assessment or stroke safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A posterior-circulation stroke is mislabelled anxiety; the treatable window is missed and the patient is left disabled.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Prompt GP review is slightly slow for acute dizziness in a 70-year-old; same-day (T3) is preferred.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Blanket ambulance for a clear hyperventilation picture with a negative stroke screen is over-triage, though safer than under-triage; same-day in-person assessment (T3) is preferred.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot perform a neurological examination or observations remotely to exclude posterior-circulation stroke in an older adult.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Reassure about the benign B12 finding and anxiety mechanism, but arrange same-day in-person assessment and give a firm stroke safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Reassure purely remotely as anxiety, or reflexively dispatch an ambulance.
